content.problemStatement:
You are given a string SSS. A cyclic shift of SSS is a string formed by moving a number of characters from the beginning of SSS to its end (in the same order). Formally, for an integer kkk (0Ōēżk<N0 \le k \lt N0Ōēżk<N), the kkk-th cyclic shift is a string RRR with length NNN such that:


Ri=Si+kR_i = S_{i+k}RiŌĆŗ=Si+kŌĆŗ for each 1ŌēżiŌēżNŌłÆk1 \le i \le N-k1ŌēżiŌēżNŌłÆk
Ri=SiŌłÆN+kR_i = S_{i-N+k}RiŌĆŗ=SiŌłÆN+kŌĆŗ for each NŌłÆk+1ŌēżiŌēżNN-k+1 \le i \le NNŌłÆk+1ŌēżiŌēżN
A string is palindromic if it is equal to its own reverse string.

Compute the number of palindromic cyclic shifts of the given string.
constraints: ŌłŻSŌłŻ|S|ŌłŻSŌłŻ contains only lowercase English letters
the sum of ŌłŻSŌłŻ|S|ŌłŻSŌłŻ in all test cases does not exceed 2Ōŗģ1052\cdot 10^52Ōŗģ105
Subtasks**Subtask #1 (20 points):** the sum of ŌłŻSŌłŻ|S|ŌłŻSŌłŻ in all test cases does not exceed 100100100
**Subtask #2 (20 points):** the sum of ŌłŻSŌłŻ|S|ŌłŻSŌłŻ in all test cases does not exceed 5,0005,0005,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single string SSS.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ŌĆö the number of palindromic shifts.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The two palindromic cyclic shifts of "aabb" are "abba" and "baab".
